title: Jumellea anjouanensis
taxonomy: Jumellea anjouanensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the genus Jumellea, which is a group of terrestrial orchids found in tropical regions. The genus Jumellea is further divided into several species, with Jumellea anjouanensis being one of the species endemic to the island of Anjouan in the Comoros. Jumellea anjouanensis is often classified as an epiphytic orchid, which means it grows on other plants or rocks, rather than in the soil. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Jumellea anjouanensis shares characteristics with other orchids, such as its unique flower structure and leaf morphology.
